requirement.original: OOS item A — bash-safety substring false positives. hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh uses substring (grep) matching to detect dangerous tokens (signal names like SIGKILL/SIGTERM, file operations like rm/unlink, etc.) anywhere in the bash command string. This produces frequent false positives when: (1) a codex prompt body passed as an argument contains documentation about dangerous operations, (2) a Python heredoc string contains bash keywords, (3) a comment line contains the token, (4) the token appears in a flag name or string argument (e.g., --no-kill-timeout). The problem: the hook cannot currently distinguish 'the token is the actual executable/operator being invoked in shell' from 'the token appears as text content in a non-executable context.' The fix: rewrite the hook's matching core to distinguish command-form context from string-content context.
requirement.clarified: Compute a COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED view of the raw bash command by stripping non-executable text spans (quoted literals, non-shell heredoc bodies, Python -c payload comments, shell comment text). Apply only the four false-positive-prone general-safety rules (killall/pkill, kill\s+-, workflow rm/mv, filesystem rm) against this stripped view. Leave ALL other rules (Layer 1.A-E daemon-restart blocks, absolute bans, sensitive-file rules, docker rules, git rules) on raw $COMMAND.
requirement.what: Add a Python context-stripping preprocessor to hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh that distinguishes executable shell context from string-content context for the four generic danger-token rules
requirement.why: Pure substring grep on the raw command string produces false positives when prompt bodies, quoted arguments, or comments contain monitored tokens. This blocked 5+ legitimate meta-audit commands during cycle 20260519-211515.

included: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh — four affected if-blocks (lines 715, 722, 743, 751); for line 743 compound condition, only the dangerous-token grep applies against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; the workflow-path grep MUST remain on raw $COMMAND (binding per M2)
excluded: Layer 1.A-E daemon-restart blocks (operate on raw $COMMAND, never stripped)

success_criteria: AC15-AC16 (regression guards for raw-COMMAND rules) all exit 2
constraints: stdlib-only Python (no bashlex or other non-stdlib packages)
Fail-safe: any parse ambiguity or exception falls back to raw command (safe side = block)
Must not apply stripped view to any rule other than the four explicitly listed
Performance: sub-100ms on typical command strings

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Hook blocks legitimate Bash commands when prompt bodies, quoted echo arguments, heredoc content, or Python -c comments contain tokens like 'kill', 'rm', 'killall', 'pkill'
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Four rules in hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh apply grep -qE to the full raw $COMMAND string with no awareness of shell quoting, heredoc context, or comment boundaries. The pattern 'kill\s+-' at line 722 matches 'kill -' anywhere in the string including inside double-quoted echo arguments. The pattern '(killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker)' at line 715 matches when 'killall' and 'claude' appear anywhere — including in a multi-line prompt body string argument to a non-shell binary.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — this is a design property of the original implementation, not a regression commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Substring grep is fast and easy to write. When each rule was introduced, the typical command being inspected was a short shell command, not a multi-KB codex prompt payload.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Modern usage includes passing large multi-line prompt bodies as string arguments to LLM CLI tools (codex, claude). These bodies routinely document forbidden operations, which means they contain the exact tokens the hook monitors.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: The false-positive class has existed since the rules were written. It became operationally significant during cycle 20260519-211515 when meta-audit commands were blocked.
observations: Line 715 grep -qE '(killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker)' applied to raw $COMMAND (inferred from source read at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:715)
Line 722 grep -qE 'kill\s+-' applied to raw $COMMAND (inferred from source read at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:722)
Line 743 grep -qE '(rm|mv)\s' applied to raw $COMMAND (inferred from source read at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:743)
Line 751 grep -qE '(^|[;|&]\s*)rm\s' applied to raw $COMMAND (inferred from source read at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:751)
Hook blocked codex invocation with prompt containing 'killall' near 'claude' — observed in this BA session (blocking of the first codex exec attempt)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (has observations — one live block observed during BA session)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Live block observed: when BA attempted 'codex exec ... "...killall...claude..."' as a shell command, hook at line 715 fired with message 'BLOCKED: Killing happy/claude/docker processes is forbidden'. This confirms the root cause: pattern matches inside the string argument to codex.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:715 (killall/pkill rule), :722 (kill -signal rule), :743 (workflow rm rule), :751 (filesystem rm rule)

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — not a regression; this is a design property of the original implementation

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: only flag dangerous tokens when they appear as: (a) the main command word of the command
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: When a dangerous token appears only inside a quoted string argument to a non-shell command, the rule does not fire (AC10, AC13, AC14)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: (b) inside a sh -c/bash -c payload argument (recursively analyzed)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: bash -c 'rm /tmp/foo' fires; bash -c 'echo safe' 'rm positional' — only the -c payload is analyzed (AC4, AC5)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: (c) as an operator in a shell heredoc's executable lines
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Heredoc piped to bash is treated as shell context (AC6, AC7); heredoc piped to tee or python3 is stripped (AC12)

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Do NOT flag tokens appearing in: # comment lines
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Shell comment text is stripped before pattern matching (AC14)
String arguments to codex, git commit -m, echo are stripped (AC10, AC13, AC14)
Heredoc bodies fed to non-shell interpreters are stripped (AC12)
The string argument to non-shell LLM CLI binaries is stripped (AC13)

component_chain.role: Four generic danger-token rules using grep -qE on raw $COMMAND — these are the only rules to be changed
Implements strip_non_executable_contexts(cmd: str) -> str using layered heuristic passes
component_chain.evidence: Will be created; inferred from integration requirement

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Blocks kill with any signal on PIDs
Blocks process killers targeting monitored services

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE '(rm|mv)\s' && echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE '(workflow-[^/]*\.json|\.claude/todos/)'; then
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Blocks deletion of workflow enforcement files

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE '(^|[;|&]\s*)rm\s' && ! echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE 'docker\s+rm\s'; then

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps / API endpoints reachable by users). PreToolUse hook runs in the dev harness only.

evidence.observed: Hook blocked 5+ legitimate meta-audit Bash commands during cycle 20260519-211515. In this BA session, codex invocation was BLOCKED when prompt body contained 'killall' near 'claude'.

measured.value: grep -qE '(killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker)' applied to raw $COMMAND including string arguments at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:715

expected.value: Rules should only fire when the dangerous token is in executable shell command position (command word, sh -c payload, shell heredoc body), not in quoted string arguments, comments, or non-shell heredoc bodies
expected.source: POSIX Shell grammar — simple command definition

evidence.gap: Rules fire on token presence anywhere in the multi-KB raw command string, including inside quoted string arguments to non-shell binaries

reference_source.description: POSIX Shell Command Language specification — shell grammar definition of simple command and word parsing

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempt at this specific fix. OOS item A from retrospective 20260519-211515 is being actioned for the first time.

context.codebase_state: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh is 1051 lines; lib/bash_context_strip.py does not exist; hooks/tests/ contains test files for allowlist, cp-checkin, and commit-strip
context.recent_commits: 1a1d058 feat(dev-cycle): Cycle 2 remediation; 50f2ca2 fix(agents): tighten style-inspector; 1bf636c chore(docs): ship cycle 20260519-211515 close-report

development_approach.strategy: 1) Create hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py implementing strip_non_executable_contexts() using four layered passes (split on control operators, strip simple-command string args, classify heredoc bodies by consumer, strip shell comments). 2) Add a Python shim in hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh immediately before line 714 to compute COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED via env-var passthrough. 3) Replace $COMMAND with $COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED in the four affected if-blocks (lines 715, 722, 743, 751). 4) Tighten kill\s+- pattern to word-boundary form. 5) Add regression test file hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py covering all 16 ACs.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA invokes the hook directly with test command strings as JSON payloads (stdin), verifies exit code matches expected (0 = allowed, 2 = blocked) for all 16 ACs. Tests can be run via pytest or direct shell invocation.

_risk_level_doc: high because this hook runs on every Bash tool call and a regression could either over-block (breaking dev workflow) or under-block (security regression). The four rules being changed include kill-signal and rm which are high-consequence if weakened.

research_findings.reason: Root cause is clear from direct code inspection and live reproduction. The algorithmic approach (layered heuristic stripping) is well-understood from the user requirement description and codex review. No best-practices web search needed.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 4 BLOCKER findings and 5 MAJOR findings. All 4 BLOCKERs were incorporated into the spec. The 5 MAJOR findings were incorporated with appropriate precision. 1 MINOR finding (scope clarification for test files) was also incorporated.

findings.issue: Applying stripped command to all grep rules can break Layer 1.A-E (Layer 1.D explicitly fires 'regardless of surrounding text')
findings.proposed_fix: Create COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED and use it only for the four generic rules; leave Layer 1.A-E and absolute bans on raw $COMMAND
findings.rationale: Fully incorporated as M3 requirement and W3/W4 non-goals

findings.issue: Heredoc classification by delimiter name is wrong — classifying by consuming command/pipeline is correct
findings.proposed_fix: Classify heredoc body by the command/pipeline consumer, not delimiter name. bash/sh/zsh <<EOF = shell context; python3<<'PY' = non-shell; cat<<EOF|bash = shell; ambiguous = preserve raw
findings.rationale: Fully incorporated as M4 and in Technical Hints Pass 3

findings.issue: Strip argv past command word will hide real commands after separators — echo 'rm text'; rm /tmp/foo would not block
findings.proposed_fix: Preprocessor must preserve top-level shell control operators; strip only within a single simple command, never subsequent commands
findings.rationale: Fully incorporated as M5, Risk 1, and AC9

findings.issue: Quoted strings can contain executable command substitutions ($() and backticks) that must still be scanned
findings.proposed_fix: In quoted strings, strip literal text but preserve and recursively analyze $() and backtick command substitutions
findings.rationale: Fully incorporated as M6, Risk 2, and AC8

findings.issue: Treating python3, node, etc. as 'non-shell argv' is too broad — python3 -c payloads are executable code
findings.proposed_fix: Conservative policy: strip Python comments only; do not strip Python string literals; fall back to raw scan on ambiguous payloads

findings.issue: sh -c / bash -c recursion needs precision: only the -c argument string is recursive, not later positional args
findings.proposed_fix: Parse enough to identify the -c command string; recursively analyze only that string; treat later argv as string/content
findings.rationale: Incorporated as M7 and Technical Hints Pass 2

findings.issue: Current regexes should become token-aware — kill\s+- should match at command-word boundary even after stripping
findings.proposed_fix: Change kill\s+- to (^|[\s;|&])(kill)\s+- to ensure word-boundary matching
findings.rationale: Incorporated as Pass 4 in Technical Hints and Risk 6

findings.issue: Some ACs (--no-kill-timeout, SIGKILL in commit message) may not actually trigger the current regexes — need confirmation
findings.proposed_fix: Confirm false positives by actual hook invocation; replace unconfirmed cases with known-false-positive test cases
findings.rationale: --no-kill-timeout does not match 'kill\s+-' (no space) so AC for that case was not added as primary FP. SIGKILL in commit message confirmed as non-triggering by pattern analysis — included as a sanity regression (AC14 uses a different form). Unconfirmed cases replaced with confirmed cases from live observation.

findings.issue: Missing MUST BLOCK tests for shell-consuming heredocs and command substitutions
findings.proposed_fix: Add AC6 (bash <<EOF with rm), AC7 (cat <<EOF | bash with kill), AC8 (echo $(rm)) and corresponding MUST NOT BLOCK AC12
findings.rationale: Added as AC6, AC7, AC8, AC12

findings.issue: Files touched ONLY hook conflicts with need for regression tests
findings.proposed_fix: Clarify: production = hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh + hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py; tests = hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.*
findings.rationale: Scope clarified in W2 and development_approach

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 BLOCKER findings and all 5 MAJOR findings were incorporated. The minor scope-clarification finding was also applied. No findings rejected.
